title: Colors
content:
### Color System Tailwind ships with an extensive color palette. Each color family has shades from 50 (lightest) to 950 (darkest): **Neutral Colors:** - **slate** - Blue-gray tones - **gray** - True gray tones - **zinc** - Slightly warm gray - **neutral** - Pure neutral gray - **stone** - Warm gray **Color Families:** - **red**, **orange**, **amber**, **yellow** - Warm colors - **lime**, **green**, **emerald** - Green spectrum - **teal**, **cyan** - Cool greens/blues - **sky**, **blue**, **indigo** - Blue spectrum - **violet**, **purple**, **fuchsia**, **pink**, **rose** - Purple/pink spectrum ### Shade Scale Each color has shades: 50, 100, 200, 300, 400, 500, 600, 700, 800, 900, 950 - **50-200**: Light backgrounds, hover states - **300-400**: Borders, muted elements - **500**: Default/base brand color - **600-700**: Darker accents, hover states for buttons - **800-900**: Text, dark backgrounds - **950**: Deepest shade ### Text Color ```html

Blue text

Dark text

``` ### Background Color ```html
Light green background
Indigo background
``` ### Border Color ```html
Light gray border
Red border
``` ### Ring Color (Focus Indicators) ```html ``` ### Custom Colors Extend the default palette in `tailwind.config.js`: ```js module.exports = { theme: { extend: { colors: { brand: { 50: '#eff6ff', 100: '#dbeafe', 500: '#3b82f6', 900: '#1e3a5f', }, }, }, }, } ``` Then use with `text-brand-500`, `bg-brand-100`, etc.
